description: Make a constitutive model(s) property assignment. Properties are name-value pairs where the name is the keyword given in the command and the value type is specific to the property. The assignment will affect any zone whose constitutive model includes the property named (noting that some properties, such as bulk , for instance, appear in many constitutive models). Limiting a property assignment to zones that have a particular constitutive model assignment is done using the range component of the command. For instance,

6.0.description: Make a constitutive model(s) property assignment. The name of the property is s and the value being assigned to it is a. The assignment will affect any zone whose constitutive model includes the property named (noting that some properties, such as bulk, for instance, appear in many consitutive models). Limiting a property assignment to zones that have a particular constitutive model assignment is done using the range component of the command. For instance, in the following:

keywords.name: elastic
keywords.description: The isotropic elastic model: properties, description.

keywords.name: othortropic
keywords.description: The orthotropic elastic model: properties, description.

keywords.name: modified-cam-clay
keywords.description: The modified Cam-Clay model: properties, description.

keywords.name: mohr-coulomb-tension
keywords.description: The Mohr-Coulomb tension (MorhT) model: properties, description.

keywords.name: plastic-hardening
keywords.description: The plastic hardening model: properties, description.

keywords.name: cap-yield-simplified
keywords.description: The simplified Cap-Yield (CHSoil) model: properties, description.

keywords.name: softening-ubiquitous
keywords.description: The bilinear strain-softening/hardening ubiquitous-joint model: properties, description.

keywords.name: strain-softening
keywords.description: The strain-softening/hardening Mohr-Coulomb model: properties, description.

keywords.name: ubiquitous-anisotropic
keywords.description: The anisotropic-elasticity ubiquitous-joint model: properties, description.

keywords.name: maxwell
keywords.description: The Maxwell (classic viscoplastic) model: properties, description.
